rxn_class_summary: Mn-catalyzed electrochemical chloroalkylation of alkenes using malononitriles/cyanoacetates and NaCl via anodically coupled electrolysis forming vicinal C-C and C-Cl bonds
electrochemical_role: Two parallel anodic oxidations mediated by Mn: (i) Mn(III) oxidizes the malononitrile (with H+/e- transfer assisted by acetate) to a transient C-centered radical, and (ii) Mn(II) oxidizes Cl- to generate a persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl chlorine-atom-transfer species; cathode reduces HOAc to H2 and OAc-

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct after IV addition to alkene (V)

electron_flow: At the anode, Mn(II)(bpy)/OAc is oxidized to Mn(III), which in parallel oxidizes the deprotonated malononitrile to a transient free C-radical IV and oxidizes Cl- to a persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl. IV adds to the alkene to form transient radical V, which abstracts Cl from [Mn(III)]-Cl (atom transfer) to deliver the chloroalkylation product; HOAc is reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode generating OAc- that returns to the anodic compartment as ligand/base.

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted alkene 36
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opening occurred after first C-C bond formation to give product 37 in 79%, confirming C-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: chlorinated malononitrile 45
probe_reagents.observation: Formed in 53% without alkene; using 45 in place of 2 gave predominantly dichloride 4 (61%) with only 32% 3, ruling out Kharasch-type pathway

probe_reagents.name: divided-cell electrolysis with NaOAc in anodic chamber
probe_reagents.observation: Restored chloroalkylation reactivity (76% at N=1.0), excluding cathode-assisted radical formation (alt. path 2)

probe_reagents.name: NaCN vs NaCl competition
probe_reagents.observation: Only chloroalkylation product (82%) observed; no cyanoalkylation, excluding free carbocation intermediate that would react with more nucleophilic CN-

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry / DPV of Mn(OTf)2/bpy with HOAc/NaOAc/Cl-/2
probe_reagents.observation: New anodic wave at ~500 mV indicates Mn(II)/Mn(III) facilitated by OAc ligand; current enhancement on adding Cl- assigned to [Mn(II)]-Cl oxidation to [Mn(III)]-Cl

notes: Two alternative pathways considered and excluded: (1) Kharasch-type Mn-catalyzed radical addition of pre-formed alpha-chloromalononitrile 45 to the alkene (ruled out by product distribution when 45 is used in place of 2, and by the no-current control); (2) cathode-assisted reduction of 45 to radical IV (ruled out by divided-cell experiment). A radical-polar crossover pathway via benzylic cation V+ and Cl- attack was also considered but disfavored by lack of acetoxylation/Ritter products and by NaCN/NaCl competition giving exclusive C-Cl product. Selectivity is rationalized by the persistent radical effect: transient IV adds fast to alkene; persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl then cross-couples with transient V.
